messages.content:

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion:
As a middle-aged man living in Chicago, I've found myself increasingly involved in conversations about COVID vaccines because of my job as a school teacher. With four children at home during the pandemic years, these discussions became part of everyday life. The first thing that comes to mind is preparation - you need facts on hand before diving into any conversation.

I remember when my own kids asked questions early on in lockdowns about why they needed shots; I made sure we had answered those from reliable sources like the CDC and WHO websites ready-to-use explanations. That approach worked better than trying to wing it or repeating what colleagues might have said incorrectly online.

In terms of methodology, being straightforward helps avoid confusion. My personality scores high on extraversion according to my Big Five profile (86), so I enjoy engaging in discussions but also understand the need for clear communication without too much jargon. When explaining how vaccines work or why public health measures matter, simple analogies help - like comparing herd immunity to a coat of protection that helps keep everyone safe.

The low agreeableness score might make some think this persona is harsh, but it means I can gently challenge misinformation when encountered. During town hall meetings about school reopening plans post-vaccination, I'd ask parents questions directly rather than softening them first, which sometimes caused more friction but led to better understanding eventually.

Interestingly, my father only had primary school education while growing up on a reservation near Oklahoma influenced how early these conversations felt natural for me. Seeing how vaccines helped keep schools running safely during tough times made their importance personal despite having low conscientiousness myself (22). But honestly? I spend most of the one hour daily on social media waiting to engage in debates anyway - so it was satisfying work.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I derived my opinion from synthesizing personal experiences as a teacher during COVID with reliable scientific information. Using simple analogies suited my high extraversion and moderate agreeableness traits balanced well with factual backing despite low conscientiousness and openness scores suggesting less formal approaches might be needed. The time on social media (roughly one hour daily) provided context for common misconceptions encountered in public discussions.
response_parsed.tone: Conversational yet authoritative, somewhat direct due to lower agreeableness but grounded in experience as a parent and educator
citations: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC)
